mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates rapidly, triggering wage growth expectations that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates a synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household balance sheet health, which amplifies corporate profit margins and lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average signals capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and earnings revisions, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite high INDPRO readings, or the historical correlation between INDPRO peaks and market downturns breaks down in a new monetary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly validates corporate earnings trajectories, causing institutional investors to adjust upward their forward cash flow estimates and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative or zero information coefficient across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows, or GDP revisions consistently lag price movements by more than 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized rate, purchasing power erosion forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases the equity risk premium and suppresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 65% of the time following CPI threshold breaches, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects synchronized strength in both employment and capital expenditure, which validates broad economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction effect disappears when GDP growth is included as a control variable.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment where real GDP growth stagnates while consumer prices accelerate, corporate profit margins face simultaneous demand destruction and input cost inflation, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50% of identified stagflationary episodes, or the joint signal fails to reject the null hypothesis of zero predictive power in a 10-year walk-forward test.
